description: A prompt template is edited inline with no versioning, so a quality regression cannot be attributed or rolled back.
body: Failure mode: someone tweaks a prompt string directly in code with no version record or eval, quality drops in production, and there is no diff to review or version to revert to. Detection signals: prompt strings edited in place; no version stamped on responses; no eval run on the change; no diff in review. Do not ship prompt edits without versioning. Safer fallback: store templates as versioned artifacts, create a new version rather than editing in place, diff and review the change, run the eval suite with a significance gate, and stamp the version on stored and production responses.
